text: REST APIs are a common way to build web services. When designing them, it's important to use clear and logical endpoints. For example, instead of `/getUser`, use `/users` for listing users and `/users/123` for a specific user. Use standard HTTP status codes like 200 for success, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. This helps clients understand the result of their requests. Always include versioning in your API, like `/v1/users`, so changes don't break existing users. Finally, write good documentation. It should explain the endpoints, expected inputs, and possible responses. Well-designed APIs are easier to use and maintain.
